questions.prompt: Which structure is best for O(1) average insert, delete, and lookup by key?
optionHints: Fixed index access; inserts/deletes are often O(n).
Expected O(1) for hash-based buckets with a good hash.
O(log n) search on sorted order.
O(n) lookup unless you keep extra indexes.
questions.explanation: Hash tables provide expected constant-time operations for key-value access when collisions are managed and the hash function spreads keys well.
Merge sort divides the array and merges in linear time per level, for log n levels → O(n log n) in all typical cases.

questions.prompt: Which pattern reduces hot-key pressure on a single cache node?
questions.context: You are scaling a read-heavy profile service. A few celebrity keys dominate traffic; cache nodes must not become single points for those keys.
optionHints: Useful for correctness, not for hot-key spread.
Spreads keys across nodes; virtual nodes reduce imbalance.
Serializes writes on one core; does not partition data.
Expensive and opposite of what caches optimize.
questions.explanation: Sharding (often with consistent hashing) distributes keys across many nodes so hot keys can be isolated or replicated without one machine owning all traffic.

questions.prompt: Why use a message queue between services?
options: To remove the need for databases
Decouple producers/consumers, buffer load, improve resilience
optionHints: Queues complement databases; they do not replace durable storage.
Async boundary: spikes absorbed, retries possible, services evolve independently.
Queues are inherently asynchronous; sync RPC is a different pattern.
Messages are usually retained for a bounded time or until processed.
questions.explanation: Message queues smooth traffic, decouple failure domains, and let downstream consumers scale at their own pace.

questions.prompt: What triggers a re-render in React function components?
options: State updates in that component or parents supplying new props/context
questions.explanation: Re-renders follow React’s state and props model (including context), not only direct DOM events.

options: Skipping expensive recalculations when deps unchanged
questions.explanation: useMemo memoizes a computed value; useEffect handles side effects, and useState holds state.
